name: ml-engineer
description: Build production ML systems with PyTorch 2.x, TensorFlow, and
capabilities: Design production-ready ML serving architectures with PyTorch 2.x, TensorFlow 2.x, and TorchServe
Implement feature stores using Feast, Tecton, or cloud-native solutions for batch and real-time features
Build distributed training pipelines with PyTorch DDP, Horovod, and DeepSpeed for multi-GPU workloads
Create model monitoring systems for data drift, model drift, and performance degradation detection
Develop A/B testing frameworks with statistical testing, gradual rollouts, and multi-armed bandits
Optimize inference performance through quantization, batching, caching, and hardware acceleration
author: sickn33
securitySummary: Prompt-only skill with no executable code. Static analysis found 0 files with executable content and computed risk score of 0/100. The SKILL.md file contains only markdown documentation and AI assistant instructions for ML engineering tasks. No security concerns identified.
valueStatement: Deploying machine learning models to production requires expertise in serving, monitoring, and infrastructure that many teams lack. This skill provides battle-tested patterns for building reliable, scalable ML systems using modern frameworks like PyTorch 2.x and TensorFlow.
userTitle: Build Production ML Systems with Expert Guidance
limitations: Provides guidance and code patterns but does not execute ML training or inference directly
Requires access to actual ML infrastructure (GPU clusters, cloud platforms) for deployment
Model performance depends on data quality and domain-specific tuning beyond this skill's scope
Cloud-specific implementations require appropriate AWS, GCP, or Azure credentials and setup

useCases.targetUser: ML engineers building consumer-facing recommendation features
useCases.title: Real-time Recommendation System
useCases.description: Design a high-throughput recommendation engine handling 100K predictions per second with Redis caching and model serving via TorchServe.

useCases.targetUser: Data platform teams implementing MLOps practices
useCases.title: ML Pipeline Automation
useCases.description: Build end-to-end ML pipelines with Apache Airflow or Kubeflow that automate data processing, training, validation, and deployment.

useCases.targetUser: ML platform engineers responsible for production model reliability
useCases.title: Model Performance Monitoring
useCases.description: Implement comprehensive monitoring with Prometheus and Grafana to track data drift, prediction latency, and business metrics in production.

promptTemplates.title: Beginner: ML Model Deployment Basics
promptTemplates.prompt: I have a trained PyTorch model saved as model.pth. Guide me through deploying it as a REST API using FastAPI and Docker. Include health checks, input validation, and basic logging.

promptTemplates.title: Intermediate: Feature Store Design
promptTemplates.prompt: Design a feature store architecture for our e-commerce recommendation system. We need both batch features (user purchase history) and real-time features (session activity). Compare Feast vs Tecton for our use case.

promptTemplates.title: Advanced: Distributed Training Strategy
promptTemplates.prompt: We need to train a 2B parameter transformer model on 8xA100 GPUs. Recommend a distributed training strategy using PyTorch FSDP or DeepSpeed. Include gradient checkpointing, mixed precision, and communication optimization.

promptTemplates.title: Expert: Production ML Monitoring
promptTemplates.prompt: Design a comprehensive monitoring system for our fraud detection model serving 10K requests/second. Include data drift detection, model performance tracking, alerting thresholds, and automated rollback triggers.

bestPractices: Always implement comprehensive input validation and data quality checks before model inference to catch drift early
Use infrastructure as code (Terraform, CloudFormation) for reproducible ML infrastructure deployments
Design for graceful degradation with fallback models and circuit breakers to maintain service during failures
antiPatterns: Deploying models without monitoring for data drift or performance degradation leads to silent failures
Hardcoding model paths or hyperparameters in application code instead of using model registries
Running training and inference on the same infrastructure causes resource contention and unpredictable latency

faq.question: Which ML frameworks does this skill support?
faq.answer: Primary support for PyTorch 2.x and TensorFlow 2.x. Also covers JAX/Flax for research workloads, scikit-learn and gradient boosting libraries for classical ML, and Hugging Face Transformers for LLM applications.

faq.question: Can this skill help with cloud-specific ML services?
faq.answer: Yes. Provides guidance for AWS SageMaker, Azure ML, GCP Vertex AI, and Databricks ML. Includes infrastructure as code templates and best practices for each platform.

faq.question: Does this skill help with model optimization for edge deployment?
faq.answer: Yes. Covers TensorFlow Lite, PyTorch Mobile, and ONNX Runtime for edge devices. Includes quantization, pruning, and distillation techniques for resource-constrained environments.

faq.question: What monitoring tools are recommended for production ML?
faq.answer: Recommends Prometheus and Grafana for infrastructure metrics, Evidently AI or WhyLabs for ML-specific monitoring, and custom business metric dashboards. Includes alerting configuration guidance.

faq.question: How does this skill handle model versioning and rollback?
faq.answer: Covers MLflow Model Registry, DVC, and Git LFS for versioning. Provides strategies for blue-green deployments, canary releases, and automated rollback based on performance thresholds.

faq.question: Can this skill help with distributed training setup?
faq.answer: Yes. Provides configurations for PyTorch DDP, FSDP, Horovod, and DeepSpeed. Covers multi-node setup, gradient accumulation, mixed precision training, and communication optimization.
